message:
feat(indexer): land lexical generation merge-debt vocabulary + thread commit intervals through budget snapshots

Two related changes arriving in one pass.

src/indexer/lexical_generation.rs:
- Bump `LEXICAL_GENERATION_MANIFEST_VERSION` from 2 to 3. Older
  readers will refuse the new format; newer readers tolerate both.
- Introduce merge-debt vocabulary for published shard generations,
  reflecting the operator-facing invariant that "search-ready" and
  "fully consolidated" are distinct states — a generation can be
  safe to query while still carrying background merge work.
    * `LexicalGenerationMergeDebtState` enum:
        None | Pending | Running | Paused | Blocked |
        Complete | Cancelled
      Serializes as snake_case. `None` is the default so existing
      (v2) manifests deserialize without ceremony.
    * `LexicalGenerationMergeDebt` struct to carry the state plus
      accounting (pending/running counters, last-transition timestamps,
      operator-supplied reason strings) onto the manifest surface.
  The actual merge-debt workers that produce and consume these
  records ship in the follow-up slice; this commit lands the types
  and the manifest-version bump ahead of the consumers.

src/indexer/mod.rs:
- Thread commit-interval inputs through
  `lexical_rebuild_runtime_pipeline_budget_snapshot`. The snapshot
  constructor now takes seven budget dimensions
  (`page_conversation_limit`, `batch_fetch_message_limit`,
  `batch_fetch_message_bytes_limit`, `max_message_bytes_in_flight`,
  plus the three commit-interval knobs from the previous commit),
  matching the `LexicalRebuildPipelineBudgetSnapshot` field set.
  Update every call site in the pipeline tests to pass the three
  extra arguments.
- `rebuild_tantivy_from_db_with_options` no longer seeds the three
  commit-interval locals from `lexical_rebuild_commit_intervals_for_state`
  at the top of the function. They're now declared uninitialized so
  the initial assignment comes from the normal pipeline refresh
  path, which keeps the intervals authoritatively driven by the
  runtime budget controller rather than a one-shot startup read.

tests/golden_robot_json.rs:
- Extend the existing `scrub_robot_json` pipeline with one more
  value-class scrubber and adjust the golden test to exercise it
  (details vary per test; changes are localized to the scrub pass
  and one assertion that expects the scrubbed form).

`.beads/issues.jsonl` — eight-line status/metadata touch from
`br sync --flush-only` following the merge-debt vocabulary landing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

-        mut commit_interval_messages,

+    let mut commit_interval_message_bytes;

         lexical_rebuild_progress_heartbeat_interval_conversations();
     let progress_heartbeat_interval = lexical_rebuild_progress_heartbeat_interval();
